heroHeading: Modular Extensions, Designed for Twickenham Homes
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions in Twickenham | EazyBase
metaDescription: Factory-built modular home extensions for Twickenham's Victorian terraces and riverside semis. Built in weeks, installed in days, designed around you.
intro:
Twickenham's streets tell a story — Victorian and Edwardian terraces running back from the river, generous semis near the green, cottages tucked into St Margarets and Strawberry Hill. They're homes people stay in. But the rooms at the back, where family life actually happens, were rarely built for the way we live now.

That's where EazyBase comes in. We design and factory-build modular home extensions in Twickenham — kitchens, dining rooms, home offices, playrooms and garden rooms — precision-made in our Blackburn facility in as little as four weeks, then installed at your home in under a week. Less time with your garden as a building site, less disruption to your street, and a finish that's checked and perfected before it ever leaves the factory.

If you've been picturing a brighter kitchen-diner opening onto the garden, or a proper room to work from, we'd love to show you how straightforward it can be.

localAngles.heading: Extending a Twickenham terrace
localAngles.body: The classic Twickenham project is the rear kitchen-diner extension — taking a narrow Victorian galley kitchen and side return and turning them into one light, sociable space that opens onto the garden. Because our extensions are engineered to precise measurements in the factory, they suit the tight tolerances of terraced plots: party walls, shared boundaries and modest gardens where every centimetre counts. We design around the character of the original house, so the new space feels like it belongs — inside and out.

localAngles.heading: Planning around conservation areas
localAngles.body: Parts of Twickenham — including areas of St Margarets and Strawberry Hill — sit within conservation areas, where extensions need a little more care over materials, rooflines and how the addition reads from the street. Elsewhere, permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions — though you should always check with the London Borough of Richmond upon Thames before starting. We'll help you understand which route applies to your home and prepare drawings that give your project the best possible footing.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works here
localAngles.body: Traditional builds on Twickenham's residential streets mean months of skips, deliveries and trades vans on roads that were never designed for them. Factory building flips that equation. Your extension takes shape in Blackburn while your home stays untouched, then arrives ready for an on-site installation measured in days, not months. For terraces with limited access and neighbours close by, it's a far kinder way to build — quieter, cleaner and dramatically shorter.

localAngles.heading: Space for riverside living
localAngles.body: Homes near the Thames, Marble Hill and Twickenham Green tend to have gardens worth making more of. A garden room gives you a home office away from the household bustle, a playroom you can see from the kitchen, or simply somewhere calm to sit as the light changes. Because the structure is made in the factory and installed quickly, your lawn and borders spend days as a worksite rather than a season — and the finished room is insulated and comfortable all year round.

faqs.q: How long does a modular extension take for a home in Twickenham?
faqs.a: Your extension is built in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks, and because that happens off-site, your home life carries on as normal in the meantime. Once it arrives in Twickenham, on-site installation typically takes under a week. Compare that with a traditional build, where the same project can mean months of disruption at the back of the house.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission in Twickenham?
faqs.a: It depends on your home and where it sits. Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions, but parts of Twickenham — including areas of St Margarets and Strawberry Hill — fall within conservation areas where additional rules apply. Always check with the London Borough of Richmond upon Thames. We'll guide you through what applies to your property and support any application with proper drawings.

faqs.q: Can you install on a terraced street with limited parking and access?
faqs.a: Yes — it's one of the situations modular building handles best. We survey your property and street before anything is built, plan delivery and craneage or access routes in advance, and coordinate any permits needed. Because installation takes days rather than months, the impact on your neighbours and your street is kept to a minimum.

faqs.q: What do Twickenham homeowners typically use their extensions for?
faqs.a: Open-plan kitchen-diners are the clear favourite — replacing a cramped Victorian kitchen with a bright, sociable space that connects to the garden. Home offices are close behind, giving commuters and hybrid workers a proper place to work. We also build dining rooms, kids' playrooms and garden rooms, each designed around how your household actually lives.
